extraction.one_line_summary: LAMPAERT Yves won from late breakaway after Team Sky triggered echelon attempt at 8km, Quick-Step countered and launched their sprinter clear.
tactical_patterns: Sky initiated echelon split at 8km despite flat sprint stage, revealing willingness to disrupt conventional sprint finishes proactively.
Quick-Step matched Sky's 60kph tempo then immediately counter-launched Lampaert solo rather than contesting bunch sprint.
Teams avoided breakaway entirely on flat stage, deliberately preserving energy for wind-driven tactical finale.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: mechanical issues resolved without consequence, team's echelon gambit failed to isolate rivals
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: capitalized on team's echelon response to win solo, also captured red jersey on stage 2
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: lost 8 seconds to Froome in echelon chaos despite being co-favorite, teammate Moreno crashed out
rider_signals.ROHAN Dennis: neutral: took third at intermediate sprint to virtually claim red jersey before losing it to Lampaert
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: Quick-Step placed two riders in winning move, showing superior positioning when Sky attacked
